_comment: Character art scene bible. Each scene has a prompt for OpenAI gpt-image-1 and an output_path. STYLE is appended automatically by generate-character-art.js.

scenes.description: Eli's reaction to Margie's Monaco postcard. The first character art piece.
scenes.scene_prompt: Eliza 'Eli' Hartwell, a woman in her early 30s with sharp observational eyes and a slightly tired but amused expression, sits at a cluttered editorial-office desk facing a window. On the wall above her desk is a glossy Mediterranean travel postcard pinned crookedly, reading 'GREETINGS FROM MONACO' in cheerful cursive. Eli's expression is dry, unimpressed, side-eyeing the postcard. In front of her on the desk: an open notebook full of tiny sketches of fire hydrants, a half-finished cup of coffee, and a typewriter. Composition: medium shot, three-quarter angle, Eli is the focal point with the postcard clearly visible as a secondary subject. Single-panel editorial-cartoon framing.
scenes.caption_text: The Staff Writer notes another postcard from the Editor-in-Chief

scenes.description: Eli portrait, neutral pose for masthead.
scenes.scene_prompt: Portrait of Eliza "Eli" Hartwell, Staff Writer at a satirical magazine, a woman in her early 30s with dark hair tied back loosely, intelligent observational eyes, slight knowing smile. She wears a wool cardigan over a button-up. She holds an open small notebook in one hand showing several ink-drawn sketches of fire hydrants in a tidy row, each clearly recognizable as a fire hydrant shape. Background: a window with city architecture visible. Composition: shoulders-up portrait, three-quarter angle, soft natural light. The fire hydrant sketches in the notebook should be the visually distinctive prop. IMPORTANT: do NOT render ANY text, labels, captions, or words ANYWHERE in the image. No name plates, no titles, no signs with words, no captions. The sketches are recognizable purely by shape, no text on the page.
scenes.caption_text: Eliza "Eli" Hartwell, Staff Writer at Thiccctionary

scenes.scene_prompt: Portrait of Bartholomew "Bart" Pruss, Senior Cataloguer at a satirical magazine, a man in his mid-50s with thinning hair, wire-rimmed glasses, an immaculately trimmed beard. He wears a tweed jacket over a vest and tie. His expression is severe and lightly disdainful. He holds a small stack of formal-looking memoranda, with a fountain pen poised over the top sheet as if about to file another objection. Background: shelves of leather-bound reference volumes. Composition: shoulders-up portrait, slight downward angle as if judging the viewer, three-quarter angle. The stack of papers + fountain pen should be the visually distinctive prop. IMPORTANT: do NOT render ANY text, labels, captions, or words ANYWHERE in the image. The papers should appear as blank typewritten forms recognizable by their formal stacked shape, no visible letters or words on them.
scenes.caption_text: Bartholomew "Bart" Pruss, Senior Cataloguer at Thiccctionary

scenes.scene_prompt: Portrait of Theodore "Teddy" Vance, Junior Cataloguer at a satirical magazine, a man in his late 20s with neatly combed hair, an earnest open face, wearing a crisp shirt and sweater vest. He holds a thick engineering manual open with a hand on the page, his other hand poised mid-gesture as if making a point. Background: a wall with a clear hand-drawn diagram of a Saturn V rocket cross-section visible behind him (recognizable by the rocket silhouette, no labels). Composition: shoulders-up portrait, three-quarter angle, warm light. The rocket diagram should be the visually distinctive prop. IMPORTANT: do NOT render ANY text, labels, captions, words, or readable diagrams ANYWHERE in the image. The Saturn V is recognizable by its iconic rocket silhouette, no text labels needed.
scenes.caption_text: Theodore "Teddy" Vance, Junior Cataloguer at Thiccctionary

scenes.scene_prompt: Portrait of Margaret "Margie" Whitmore-Hessian, Editor-in-Chief of a satirical magazine, a woman in her mid-60s with silver hair styled in a soft chignon, an expensive silk blouse, large pearl earrings, an expression of confident vagueness. She holds a wine glass in one hand and gestures vaguely with the other mid-anecdote. Background: a sunny terrace overlooking a vineyard with rows of grapevines visible, slightly blurred. Composition: shoulders-up portrait, slight upward angle, golden hour light. The wine glass + vineyard backdrop should be the visually distinctive setting. IMPORTANT: do NOT render ANY text, labels, captions, or words ANYWHERE in the image. No vineyard signs, no wine bottle labels, no nameplates.

Portrait of Hugh Drumm, Voice Editor at a satirical magazine, a man of indeterminate age between 40 and 60 with a perfectly neutral expression. Pale skin, very deliberately combed hair, monochrome shirt buttoned to the top, no tie. He holds a small open round tin of throat lozenges in one hand, the tin tipped slightly toward the viewer so we can clearly see several small round disc-shaped lozenges nestled inside it. He looks directly at the viewer with no apparent emotion. Background: an empty acoustic recording booth, slightly visible foam paneling, a studio microphone on a boom arm just visible at one edge. Composition: shoulders-up portrait, dead-center symmetrical framing, flat lighting. The open tin of visible round lozenges should be UNMISTAKABLY the lozenges (small flat round candies in a tin). IMPORTANT: do NOT render ANY text, labels, captions, or words ANYWHERE in the image. No brand name on the tin, no studio sign, no microphone label. The lozenges are recognizable purely by their shape: small round flat discs in a round metal tin.
scenes.caption_text: Hugh Drumm, Voice Editor at Thiccctionary

scenes.description: Spider Hennessy portrait — but a SILHOUETTE, since no one has met them.
scenes.scene_prompt: Silhouette portrait of "Spider" Hennessy, the Field Correspondent for a satirical magazine whom no one in the office has met. The figure is in heavy shadow against a backlit window, wearing a wide-brimmed hat and a long coat. Only the outline is visible. In the foreground on a desk: a small postcard showing a stylized Macau skyline (recognizable as a city with distinctive Asian harbor architecture), and a sheet of paper next to it that is clearly an expense report form by its formal columnar layout. Composition: shoulders-up backlit silhouette, dramatic chiaroscuro, mysterious atmosphere. The postcard + expense form should be visually clear by shape and layout alone. IMPORTANT: do NOT render ANY text, labels, captions, or words ANYWHERE in the image. The postcard is recognizable by the skyline silhouette, the expense form by its columnar grid layout.
scenes.caption_text: "Spider" Hennessy, the Thiccctionary Field Correspondent, whom no one in the office has met
